2605.22717.title: Live Music Diffusion Models: Efficient Fine-Tuning and Post-Training of Interactive Diffusion Music Generators
2605.22717.paper_summary: Interactive streaming music generation promises the use of generative models for live performance and co-creation that is impossible with offline models. However, SOTA models exist in the discrete-AR regime, requiring industrial levels of compute for both training and inference. In this work, we investigate whether audio diffusion models, with their wide support in the open-source community but non-streaming bidirectional nature, can be repurposed efficiently into interactive models accessible on consumer hardware. By taking a critical look at the modern pipeline for block-wise outpainting diffusion, we identify critical inefficiencies during inference that result in strictly worse computational efficiency than their discrete-AR counterparts. We propose Live Music Diffusion Models (LMDMs), a simple modification of the generative diffusion process that recovers, and then outperforms, the inference complexity of the discrete Live Music Models (LMMs) through block-wise KV Caching. Unlike LMMs, LMDMs further enable stable post-training alignment through our novel ARC-Forcing paradigm, reducing error accumulation without any explicit RL or reward models. We demonstrate the application of LMDMs in a number of creative domains, including text-conditioned generation, sketch-based music synthesis, and jamming. We finally show how LMDMs can be used as a generative instrument in a real artist-AI collaboration, utilizing LMDMs as a "generative delay" to transform musicians' improvisation live for variable timbral effects while running locally on a consumer gaming laptop.
2605.22717.paper_summary_zh: 互動式串流音樂生成承諾使用生成模型進行現場表演和共同創作，這在離線模型中是無法實現的。然而，最先進的模型存在於離散自回歸（discrete-AR）範疇，對於訓練和推斷都需要工業級別的計算資源。在本研究中，我們探討音頻擴散模型是否可以有效地重新利用，儘管它們在開源社區中得到廣泛支持，但其非串流的雙向特性使其難以轉化為可在消費者硬體上訪問的互動模型。通過對現代塊狀擴散的管道進行批判性分析，我們識別出推斷過程中的關鍵低效，這導致其計算效率明顯低於離散自回歸模型。我們提出了現場音樂擴散模型（LMDMs），這是一種對生成擴散過程的簡單修改，通過塊狀鍵值緩存（KV Caching）恢復並超越離散現場音樂模型（LMMs）的推斷複雜度。與LMMs不同，LMDMs進一步通過我們的新穎ARC-Forcing範式實現穩定的後訓練對齊，減少了錯誤累積，而不需要任何明確的強化學習或獎勵模型。我們展示了LMDMs在多個創意領域的應用，包括文本條件生成、草圖音樂合成和即興演奏。我們最終展示了如何將LMDMs用作真實藝術家與AI合作中的生成工具，利用LMDMs作為“生成延遲”，即時轉換音樂家的即興演奏，以實現可變音色效果，同時在消費者遊戲筆記本上本地運行。
2605.22717.author: Zachary Novack et.al.
2605.22717.authors: Zachary Novack, Stephen Brade, Haven Kim, Hugo Flores García, Nithya Shikarpur, Chinmay Talegaonkar, Suwan Kim, Valerie K. Chen, Julian McAuley, Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick, Cheng-Zhi Anna Huang
